rationale:
Intuition: recipe_cook.html's ingredient highlighting uses client-side substring matching (tokenize ingredient names, call stepText.includes(token)). This fails for step references like "the dough", "the sauce", "remaining mixture" that share no substring with raw ingredient names, and over-matches when ingredients share common words. The model has no recipe-wide context at render time.

What I'd fix: precompute a step→ingredient index map once per recipe using haiku, cache it in the DB, and serve it to the frontend. The model sees the full ingredient list + all steps in one shot so it can resolve semantic references correctly.

How: (1) add step_ingredient_map TEXT column to recipes table with try/except migration in db.py; (2) new GET /api/recipes/<id>/cook_map Flask endpoint that computes via haiku on first call, persists, returns cached on subsequent; (3) update recipe_cook.html to fetch the map async and use it in the highlight function instead of substring logic.
